title: Focus & Clarity
subtitle: Clear the noise. Sharpen your mind.

cues.text:
Sit upright, feet flat, back supported, hands on your thighs.
Close your eyes.

Take three grounding breaths.
In through the nose.

Bring your attention to the center of your forehead.
Just notice that space.
No effort needed, just a gentle awareness.

Now think about what you're about to do.
The work. The task. The conversation ahead.
Don't think about how, or when.
Just hold a clear picture of what it is.

With each inhale, imagine breathing in clarity.
Clean, clear air, filling your mind.

With each exhale, let go of anything that isn't relevant right now.
The open tabs. The other tasks. The background noise.

Your mind knows how to do this.
It's been trained for this.
Right now, you're just pointing it in the right direction.

If a thought pulls you sideways, acknowledge it briefly, then let it go.
Return to that clear picture of your task.

Feel yourself becoming more present.
More awake.

As you exhale, feel yourself arrive completely in this moment.
Fully alert.
